channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: US military conducts airstrikes against Islamic State operatives in Somalia
item.description: The U.S. military has conducted coordinated airstrikes against Islamic State operatives in Somalia, the first attacks in the African nation during President Donald Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China could mean higher inflation and economic disruption
item.description: New trade penalties against Canada, Mexico and China that President Donald Trump plans to impose represent an aggressive early move against America’s three largest trading partners, but at the risk of higher inflation and possible disruptions to the global economy.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Political parties explain Hillsborough County's conservative flip
item.description: As of Thursday, Hillsborough County records showed the number of active registered Republicans was bigger than the number of registered Democrats. The difference as of Friday was 307 voters.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Woman who wanted to kill GOP leaders said she was inspired by CEO's slaying, prosecutors say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Massachusetts woman who told police that she went to the U.S. Capitol to kill members of President Donald Trump's cabinet said she was influenced by Luigi Mangione, the man charged with fatally shooting the CEO of UnitedHealthcare, prosecutors said in a court filing.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump official travels to Venezuela in push for Maduro regime to take back Tren de Aragua gang members
item.description: Ric Grenell has traveled to Venezuela to meet with socialist dictator Nicolas Maduro and demand that he accept deported criminals from the U.S. and release American hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants to cut the federal workforce. Who they are and what that means
item.description: President Trump’s efforts to cut the federal workforce by 10 percent might not save as much money as hoped and could have unintended consequences. NPR explains why.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman, MPR News

item.title: Trump makes his first moves on education, but no McMahon hearing in sight
item.description: President Trump made his first major moves on education Wednesday, signing a series of executive orders that come even before his nominee to lead the Education Department, Linda McMahon, has had her Senate confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Senator calls RFK Jr.’s position on race and vaccines dangerous
item.description: In one of the most tense exchanges in a heated confirmation hearing, Senator Angela Alsobrooks called out past comments RFK Jr. made suggesting a different vaccine schedule for Black people.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: What to know about the legal challenges to Trump’s executive actions
item.description: President Trump’s torrent of executive actions has spurred a flood of legal challenges seeking to thwart the president’s efforts to reshape U.S. policy in his first days back in the White House. From Seattle to Boston and Concord, N.H., to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans leave Florida frustrated with lack of final plan to pass Trump agenda
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) arrived at this week’s House Republican retreat with hopes of uniting the fractious GOP conference around a plan to pass President Trump's agenda — but instead, the group is departing south Florida with rising tensions in its ranks. Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Doug Burgum as interior secretary after Trump tasked him to boost drilling
item.description: The Senate confirmed Doug Burgum as interior secretary late Thursday after President Donald Trump tapped the North Dakota billionaire to spearhead the Republican administration's ambitions to boost fossil fuel production. The vote was 79-18.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: GOP senator 'surprised' Gabbard refused to call Snowden a traitor
item.description: Sen. James Lankford (R-Okla.), a member of the Senate Intelligence Committee, said he was “surprised” that director of national intelligence nominee Tulsi Gabbard refused to call former NSA contractor Edward Snowden a “traitor” after being repeatedly pressed on the question at a Thursday hearing.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Trump blames diversity hiring as probe into deadly DC plane crash begins
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the nation reeled from the deadliest American aviation disaster in more than two decades, President Donald Trump on Thursday baselessly blamed diversity initiatives for undermining air safety and questioned the actions of a U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who is Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy? You may recognize him from MTV
item.description: Duffy, formerly a reality TV star and Wisconsin congressman, was confirmed as the new transportation secretary on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin, Fox 5

item.title: Rubio says Trump’s interest in Greenland and Panama Canal is legit and driven by China concerns
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio says President Donald Trump’s desire to acquire Greenland and retake control of the Panama Canal is driven by legitimate national security interests stemming from growing concerns about Chinese activity and influence in the Arctic and in Latin America
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Senators grill 3 of Trump's most controversial Cabinet picks in confirmation hearings
item.description: Three of President Donald Trump’s cabinet picks faced skepticism and intense grilling from Democratic senators during their confirmation hearings on Thursday. What we’re following: 1. Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President Trump casts blame during briefing on deadly plane crash
item.description: President Donald Trump started Thursday’s press briefing with a moment of silence for the victims and their families after a deadly crash involving an American Airlines plane and an Army helicopter near Ronald Reagan National Airport on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump blames diversity hiring as probe into deadly D.C. plane crash begins
item.description: As the nation reeled from the deadliest American aviation disaster in more than two decades, President Donald Trump on Thursday baselessly blamed diversity initiatives for undermining air safety and questioned the actions of a U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Hundreds protest immigration policies at Trump Hotel in Vegas
item.description: Around 200 people gathered outside the Trump Hotel in Vegas to push back against the administration's immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Sasha Loftis, NewsNation

item.title: Trump suggests FAA's diversity efforts caused safety issues in crash
item.description: Trump weighed in on a crash between an American Airlines plane and a military helicopter, claiming it “should have been prevented.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard, RFK Jr., Patel hearings set to consume Capitol Hill
item.description: Hearing-mania is set to consume Capitol Hill on Thursday as a trio of President Trump's top allies appear for high-stakes confirmation showdowns with senators that could make or break their chances of getting across the finish line. Tulsi Gabbard, Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trans community fears Trump's actions will upend legal precedent on prison protections
item.description: One of President Trump's first executive orders impacts transgender inmates incarcerated in federal facilities. It's just one in a series of actions signed by Trump that target the trans community.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz, MPR News

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right health recommendations
item.description: The latest KFF survey finds 42 percent of respondents say they have either a great deal or fair amount of trust in Trump to make the right recommendations when it comes to health issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Center for Victims of Torture dramatically slashes workforce after Trump administration orders
item.description: President Donald Trump’s executive orders forced many international humanitarian organizations, like St. Paul-based Center for Victims of Torture, to cut spending and furlough staff.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Voters to receive postcards for District 1 special primary election
item.description: Registered voters in San Diego County's First Supervisorial District will soon receive postcards for the April 8 special primary election.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Trump addresses House Republicans at Florida retreat: 5 takeaways
item.description: DORAL, Fla. — President Trump floated running for a third term, commented on a new Chinese artificial intelligence app, and urged House Republicans to unify and quickly advance his legislative priorities in a speech to the lawmakers at their annual policy retreat.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5
